name: Elsie's Gingerbread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk (measure into a 2-cup measuring cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spray an 8x11-inch baking dish with Pam and the bowl of a tablespoon measure.
3. Set both aside.
4. Combine flour, sugar, ginger, and cinnamon in a bowl.
5. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in shortening until crumbly.
6. Scoop out about 1/3 cup of this mixture and set aside for topping.
7. In the buttermilk measure, add baking soda and salt, stirring to combine. It will foam slightly.
8. Set aside.
9. Pour the beaten egg into the larger amount of topping.
10. Add molasses, measuring it in the Pam-sprayed tablespoon, then pour directly into the batter.
11. Stir the batter a few times, then add the buttermilk mixture.
12. Stir to blend ingredients; lumps are okay.
13. Turn the batter into the prepared baking dish and sprinkle with topping.
14. Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center tests clean.
15. Serve warm, if possible, plain or with whipped cream, ice cream, frozen yogurt, or baked apple.
